situation: Derived fix from audit-2026-07-08-046. post_xhs_browseruse.py writes publish-record JSON via open('w')+json.dump — non-atomic. Crash mid-write or concurrent cron can leave truncated/corrupt record file. Severity medium.
action: Replace direct open('w') with tempfile.mkstemp(dir=POSTS_DIR)+os.fdopen+json.dump+os.replace pattern. Cleanup temp file on any BaseException. Added 'import tempfile'. Single-file route-fix in existing opt-scripts worktree.
outcome_evidence: Commit 6448d32 on auto/opt-scripts-1783396445 pushed to origin. Diff: +12/-2 lines. tempfile.mkstemp creates .tmp in POSTS_DIR, os.replace atomically renames to target. Exception handler unlinks temp on failure.
next_suggestion: Dispatch SP-SCR-L-002 (wanxia-mail-push.sh MAIL_TO unbound under set -u)
Dispatch SP-SCR-L-004 (xhs-daily.js exec URL template unescaped in shell)
Dispatch SP-ENG-L-002 (weather-fetcher.js extractSunsetWindowData assumes daily.sunset[0] exists)

audit_findings.finding: Non-atomic publish-record JSON write: open('w')+json.dump can produce truncated file on crash or concurrent cron execution
audit_findings.remediation: Use tempfile.mkstemp+os.replace for atomic write; cleanup temp on exception
